PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Soo Hoo in Propria Persona.

The Court asked Mr. Soo Hoo if he is familiar with what the next steps are. Mr. Soo Hoo stated that he has engaged a CPA to set up a checking account, that he needs to gather the assets and that he needs to get a tax identification number. The Court expressed concern about self-represented Parties knowledge and ability to follow the probate procedures. Mr. Soo Hoo stated that his Cousin is an Attorney and that the person who has assisted him to this point is also an Attorney. The Court stated that it is inclined to allow the petition, but set bond for the full amount of the assets in the estate. Mr. Soo Hoo stated that his Sister did not own real property, that she did not have a car and that most of her accounts were transferred to the Beneficiaries of those accounts. The Court informed Mr. Soo Hoo of the requirement to do an inventory and appraisal of the assets, and the need to have the letters issued. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $282,000.00 is required. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for November 9, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for October 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Soo Hoo of the importance of tracking said hearing dates.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
